strategy: Fire boss: hit with Ice, the only school he takes extra from (+5%); Fire is resisted 73%. Neither he nor the White Tiger can be stunned.
Equalize caps all damage at 3,000, so forget one-shots and plan to chip both enemies down under the cap.
Never hit with an all-enemies spell: Power Recharge heals every enemy to full when he is hit by AoE. Use single-target hits, or a drain AoE like Scarecrow, which does not trigger it.
Every third round Power Stunner stuns your team for a round and adds -40% accuracy, so bring stun blocks and accuracy.
Hitting the White Tiger resets and delays its ROAR (Insane Bolt, up to 1,550 Moon to your team), so chip the Tiger to keep pushing that nuke back.
Grind both enemies down under the 3,000 cap and finish.
cheatNotes: Join by Round 2 or he hits the latecomer with Impede.
Equalize caps every hit at 3,000 damage, so big single-shot kills are off the table; plan to chip.
Trying to swap out his Equalize global just makes him recast it, so leave it.
Every third round Power Stunner stuns your whole team for a round and adds -40% accuracy; pack stun blocks.
Hitting him with any all-enemies spell heals every enemy to full, so only ever use single-target hits here.
Drain AoEs like Scarecrow are the exception and do not trigger the full heal.
A late wizard also eats a Sabertooth from the White Tiger.
When the Tiger says WARNING ROAR, its big ROAR nuke lands next turn.
ROAR is a Moon Insane Bolt to your whole team for 550, 1,050, or 1,550 every two rounds.
Hitting the Tiger delays ROAR and resets its two-round timer, so chip it to stall the nuke.
SNARL means the Tiger Empowered itself; watch for a stronger follow-up.
Putting a negative charm on the Tiger triggers that Empower.
The Tiger also Empowers after it casts Grim Reader or after Draconian casts Lord of the Jungle.
